fields.title: Expectation-oriented specification contract and store-owned evidence workflow
Expectation-oriented specification contract and store-owned evidence workflow

acceptance_criteria.statement: The migrated spec defines its workflow contract in terms of native ownership and not wrapper-only rollout artifacts.
The migrated spec names the evidence needed to show when the workflow contract is satisfied or blocked.
Any referenced tool surfaces are treated as thin adapters over shared workflow behavior, not as separate workflow products.

evidence_requirements.description: The authored spec body documents the native workflow contract, explicit evidence expectations, and any shared-surface responsibilities.
Spec health and audit validation confirm the migrated workflow contract remains structurally sound.

expected_properties.statement: Workflow-facing metadata and evidence links are owned by native stores or shared-library contracts instead of wrapper-only artifacts.
Expected properties, acceptance clauses, and required evidence are explicit enough to evaluate the workflow contract.
CLI, MCP, and HTTP surfaces remain thin adapters over shared workflow behavior where applicable.

fulfillment_summaries.detail: The migrated spec body and stored contract fields now describe the workflow expectation contract without relying on wrapper-only rollout prose.
Focused spec health and audit validation completed after the homogeneous workflow-spec migration.

fields.title: Expectation-oriented specification contract and store-owned evidence workflow
